relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Bezalel makes the ark from acacia wood.
Gold covers the ark within and without.
Rings are cast for carrying.
Gold-covered poles are made.
The poles carry the ark of presence.
The pure gold cover is made.
Two hammered cherubim rise from the cover.
Cherubim emerge from both ends.
Their wings shelter the meeting place.
The table is made from acacia wood.
Gold surrounds the table.
A rim and molding guard its edge.
Rings are set on its corners.
The rings receive carrying poles.
The table is made portable.
Golden vessels serve the table.
The lampstand is hammered from pure gold.
Six branches extend from its sides.
Almond blossoms open along the branches.
The center blooms with cups, bulbs, and blossoms.
Bulbs gather beneath the paired branches.
The lampstand is one hammered work.
Seven lamps and tending tools are made.
A talent of gold becomes the light-tree and vessels.
The incense altar is shaped from acacia wood.
Gold covers its top, sides, horns, and molding.
Rings make the incense altar portable.
Gold-covered poles are made.
Holy oil and fragrant incense are blended by the perfumer.

contemplative_note: Exodus 37 is holy pattern made tangible: ark, cover, cherubim, table, golden tree of light, incense altar, oil, and fragrance.
